rubrics.name: Standard Technical Question Rubric
rubrics.description: Applied to all technical interview questions. Weights concept coverage highest because technical accuracy is the primary concern.

criteria.name: Concept Coverage
criteria.description: How many of the required technical concepts were correctly mentioned and explained.

scoring_guide.0-25: None or very few required concepts mentioned. Answer appears to be guessing or is entirely off-topic.
scoring_guide.26-50: Some required concepts mentioned but major concepts are missing or incorrectly described.
scoring_guide.51-75: Most required concepts mentioned but one or two important ones are missing or insufficiently explained.
scoring_guide.76-100: All or nearly all required concepts mentioned with correct explanations. Optional bonus concepts may also be present.

criteria.name: Semantic Relevance
criteria.description: How closely the overall meaning and direction of the answer aligns with the expected answer.

scoring_guide.0-25: Answer is largely irrelevant or answers a different question entirely.
scoring_guide.26-50: Answer is tangentially related but misses the main point.
scoring_guide.51-75: Answer is relevant and addresses the question but misses some important nuances.
scoring_guide.76-100: Answer is highly relevant and aligns closely with the expected response in both content and direction.

criteria.name: Reasoning Depth
criteria.description: How deeply the candidate explains the why and how behind their answer, not just the what.

scoring_guide.0-25: Answer states facts or keywords with no explanation of why or how.
scoring_guide.26-50: Some causal explanation present but mostly surface-level statements.
scoring_guide.51-75: Good explanatory depth with some causal or comparative reasoning.
scoring_guide.76-100: Excellent depth with strong causal reasoning, comparative analysis, or internal system explanation.

criteria.name: Evidence and Examples
criteria.description: Whether the candidate provided concrete real-world examples or specific technology references.

scoring_guide.0-25: No examples or references. Purely theoretical or abstract.
scoring_guide.26-50: Vague reference to examples without specifics.
scoring_guide.51-75: At least one concrete example or technology reference provided.
scoring_guide.76-100: Multiple specific examples with real-world context demonstrating practical experience.

criteria.name: Communication Quality
criteria.description: Clarity of explanation, sentence structure, grammar, and appropriate use of technical vocabulary.

scoring_guide.0-25: Very difficult to understand. Poor grammar, incoherent structure.
scoring_guide.26-50: Understandable but poorly organized with noticeable grammar issues.
scoring_guide.51-75: Clear and readable with minor structural issues.
scoring_guide.76-100: Excellent clarity, well-structured, appropriate technical vocabulary, easy to follow.
